notes: 24 uniform 5-vertex LWPOLYLINE quads on X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL; insulation fill units, no discrete wall-boundary lines.

rationale.evidence: All 24 sampled entities (handles 4F0D-4F24) are LWPOLYLINE with exactly 5 vertices, 100% on layer X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL. There is no LINE, WALL-tagged layer, or bbox data; the entity type/layer histograms are perfectly homogeneous, which matches a tiled fill pattern rather than an outline.
rationale.rule: A layer suffix naming a material (INSUL = insulation) rather than a structural element (벽/WALL), combined with many identically-shaped small closed polylines, indicates a fill/hatch-simulation series for that material and not the wall boundary line itself.
rationale.counterfactual: If the layer name instead ended in 벽체/WALL, or if a subset of entities were simple 2-point LINE segments at a consistent offset (wall thickness), wall_likelihood and wall_line_handles would both rise.
rationale.uncertainty: No bbox or vertex coordinates are given, so the aspect ratio of each quad and whether the 24 units tile along a straight wall run (versus a curved or isolated feature) cannot be confirmed from this projection.

notes: Flagpole-stand plan symbol (INSERT of X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기계양대 block) built from a 20-LINE curb/tab outline plus 2 WIPEOUT masks; named as equipment, not a building wall.

rationale.evidence: dxf_name histogram is INSERT=1, LINE=20, WIPEOUT=2; the INSERT (handle 52A0) explicitly references block X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기계양대 (flagpole hoisting platform), directly naming the object. The 20 LINE entities trace a frame within bbox [-610,-103.5]-[610,22.5], including a long 1176-unit band at y=-50.5/-100.5 (handles 52A1/52A3) that is re-traced by near-duplicate reversed segments (52A9/52B1) — a double-line drafting convention normally used for wall/curb thickness — plus short tab returns (e.g. 52A5, 52AD) forming two small wings, and 2 WIPEOUT entities used to mask overlapping symbol geometry.
rationale.rule: When an INSERT directly names a specific equipment/fixture block, that explicit label takes priority over incidental resemblance of the surrounding LINE geometry to a double-line wall; WIPEOUT usage is also a strong tell for a graphic symbol rather than raw structural drafting.
rationale.counterfactual: Without the self-naming INSERT (국기계양대) and the WIPEOUT masks, the duplicate-traced 1176x50 band and corner tabs would read far more plausibly as an actual small curb/plinth wall rather than a symbol's own base outline.
rationale.uncertainty: Whether the duplicate-traced band represents a real physical curb/footing around the flagpole (and thus should itself count as a short wall) or is purely a stylized graphic border cannot be confirmed without seeing the referenced 국기계양대 block's own contents or a hatch/material tag.
